    impl From<SvnErrnoReposRa> for ffi::svn_errno_t {
        fn from(value: SvnErrnoReposRa) -> Self {
            match value {
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposLocked => ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_LOCKED,
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposHookFailure => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_HOOK_FAILURE
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposBadArgs => ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_BAD_ARGS,
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposNoDataForReport => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_NO_DATA_FOR_REPORT
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposBadRevisionReport => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_BAD_REVISION_REPORT
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposUnsupportedVersion => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_UNSUPPORTED_VERSION
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposDisabledFeature => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_DISABLED_FEATURE
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposPostCommitHookFailed => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_POST_COMMIT_HOOK_FAILED
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposPostLockHookFailed => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_POST_LOCK_HOOK_FAILED
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposPostUnlockHookFailed => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_POST_UNLOCK_HOOK_FAILED
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposUnsupportedUpgrade => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_UNSUPPORTED_UPGRADE
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaIllegalUrl => ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_ILLEGAL_URL,
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaNotAuthorized => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_NOT_AUTHORIZED
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaUnknownAuth => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_UNKNOWN_AUTH
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaNotImplemented => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_NOT_IMPLEMENTED
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaOutOfDate => ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_OUT_OF_DATE,
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaNoReposUuid => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_NO_REPOS_UUID
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaUnsupportedAbiVersion => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_UNSUPPORTED_ABI_VERSION
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaNotLocked => ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_NOT_LOCKED,
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaPartialReplayNotSupported => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_PARTIAL_REPLAY_NOT_SUPPORTED
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaUuidMismatch => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_UUID_MISMATCH
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaReposRootUrlMismatch => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_REPOS_ROOT_URL_MISMATCH
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaSessionUrlMismatch => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_SESSION_URL_MISMATCH
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaCannotCreateTunnel => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_CANNOT_CREATE_TUNNEL
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaCannotCreateSession => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_CANNOT_CREATE_SESSION
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavSockInit => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_SOCK_INIT
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavCreatingRequest => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_CREATING_REQUEST
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavRequestFailed => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_REQUEST_FAILED
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavOptionsReqFailed => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_OPTIONS_REQ_FAILED
                }
                SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavPropsNotFound => {
                    ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_PROPS_NOT_FOUND
                }
            }
        }
    }
    impl TryFrom<ffi::svn_errno_t> for SvnErrnoReposRa {
        type Error = ();
        fn try_from(value: ffi::svn_errno_t) -> Result<Self, Self::Error> {
            match value {
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_LOCKED => Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposLocked),
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_HOOK_FAILURE => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposHookFailure)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_BAD_ARGS => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposBadArgs)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_NO_DATA_FOR_REPORT => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposNoDataForReport)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_BAD_REVISION_REPORT => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposBadRevisionReport)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_UNSUPPORTED_VERSION => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposUnsupportedVersion)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_DISABLED_FEATURE => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposDisabledFeature)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_POST_COMMIT_HOOK_FAILED => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposPostCommitHookFailed)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_POST_LOCK_HOOK_FAILED => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposPostLockHookFailed)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_POST_UNLOCK_HOOK_FAILED => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposPostUnlockHookFailed)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_REPOS_UNSUPPORTED_UPGRADE => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::ReposUnsupportedUpgrade)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_ILLEGAL_URL => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaIllegalUrl)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_NOT_AUTHORIZED => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaNotAuthorized)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_UNKNOWN_AUTH => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaUnknownAuth)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_NOT_IMPLEMENTED => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaNotImplemented)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_OUT_OF_DATE => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaOutOfDate)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_NO_REPOS_UUID => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaNoReposUuid)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_UNSUPPORTED_ABI_VERSION => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaUnsupportedAbiVersion)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_NOT_LOCKED => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaNotLocked)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_PARTIAL_REPLAY_NOT_SUPPORTED => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaPartialReplayNotSupported)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_UUID_MISMATCH => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaUuidMismatch)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_REPOS_ROOT_URL_MISMATCH => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaReposRootUrlMismatch)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_SESSION_URL_MISMATCH => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaSessionUrlMismatch)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_CANNOT_CREATE_TUNNEL => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaCannotCreateTunnel)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_CANNOT_CREATE_SESSION => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaCannotCreateSession)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_SOCK_INIT => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavSockInit)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_CREATING_REQUEST => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavCreatingRequest)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_REQUEST_FAILED => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavRequestFailed)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_OPTIONS_REQ_FAILED => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavOptionsReqFailed)
                }
                ffi::svn_errno_t_SVN_ERR_RA_DAV_PROPS_NOT_FOUND => {
                    Ok(SvnErrnoReposRa::RaDavPropsNotFound)
                }
                _ => Err(()),
            }
        }

    unsafe extern "C" fn may_save_password_as_plain_text(
        save: *mut ffi::svn_boolean_t,
        realm_string: *const c_char,
        baton: *mut c_void,
        pool: *mut ffi::apr_pool_t,
    ) -> *mut ffi::svn_error_t {
        let ctx = unsafe { &mut *(baton as *mut ContextInner) };
        unsafe {
            let realm_string = CStr::from_ptr(realm_string).to_str().unwrap();
            *save = ctx
                .context_notifier
                .may_save_password_as_plain_text(realm_string.to_string())
                .into();
        }
        std::ptr::null_mut()
    }
    unsafe extern "C" fn first_ssl_client_cert_pw() -> *mut ffi::svn_error_t {
        svn_no_error()
    }
    unsafe extern "C" fn info_receiver(
        baton: *mut c_void,
        path: *const c_char,
        info: *const ffi::svn_client_info2_t,
        scratch_pool: *mut apr::ffi::apr_pool_t,
    ) -> *mut ffi::svn_error_t {
        unsafe {
            let context = (baton as *mut ContextInner).as_mut().unwrap();
        }
        svn_no_error()
    }
    pub struct SslServerCertInfo {
        hostname: String,
        fingerprint: String,
        valid_from: String,
        valid_until: String,
        issuer: String,
        ascii_cert: String,
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    impl ::core::fmt::Debug for SslServerCertInfo {
        #[inline]
        fn fmt(&self, f: &mut ::core::fmt::Formatter) -> ::core::fmt::Result {
            let names: &'static _ = &[
                "hostname",
                "fingerprint",
                "valid_from",

        impl InternalBitFlags {
            /// Get a flags value with all bits unset.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn empty() -> Self {
                Self(<u32 as ::bitflags::Bits>::EMPTY)
            }
            /// Get a flags value with all known bits set.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn all() -> Self {
                let mut truncated = <u32 as ::bitflags::Bits>::EMPTY;
                let mut i = 0;
                {
                    {
                        let flag = <SslFailures as ::bitflags::Flags>::FLAGS[i]
                            .value()
                            .bits();
                        truncated = truncated | flag;
                        i += 1;
                    }
                };

                        let flag = <SslFailures as ::bitflags::Flags>::FLAGS[i]
                            .value()
                            .bits();
                        truncated = truncated | flag;
                        i += 1;
                    }
                };

                            .value()

                            .bits();
                        truncated = truncated | flag;
                        i += 1;
                    }
                };
                let _ = i;
                Self(truncated)
            }
            /// Get the underlying bits value.
            ///
            /// The returned value is exactly the bits set in this flags value.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn bits(&self) -> u32 {
                self.0
            }
            /// Convert from a bits value.
            ///
            /// This method will return `None` if any unknown bits are set.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn from_bits(
                bits: u32,
            ) -> ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option<Self> {
                let truncated = Self::from_bits_truncate(bits).0;
                if truncated == bits {
                    ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(Self(bits))
                } else {
                    ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::None
                }
            }
            /// Convert from a bits value, unsetting any unknown bits.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn from_bits_truncate(bits: u32) -> Self {
                Self(bits & Self::all().0)
            }
            /// Convert from a bits value exactly.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn from_bits_retain(bits: u32) -> Self {
                Self(bits)
            }
            /// Get a flags value with the bits of a flag with the given name set.
            ///
            /// This method will return `None` if `name` is empty or doesn't
            /// correspond to any named flag.
            #[inline]
            pub fn from_name(
                name: &str,
            ) -> ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option<Self> {
                {
                    if name == "NOTYETVALID" {
                        return ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(
                            Self(SslFailures::NOTYETVALID.bits()),
                        );
                    }
                };
                {
                    if name == "EXPIRED" {
                        return ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(
                            Self(SslFailures::EXPIRED.bits()),
                        );
                    }
                };
                {
                    if name == "CNMISMATCH" {
                        return ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(
                            Self(SslFailures::CNMISMATCH.bits()),
                        );
                    }
                };
                {
                    if name == "UNKNOWNCA" {
                        return ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(
                            Self(SslFailures::UNKNOWNCA.bits()),
                        );
                    }
                };
                {
                    if name == "OTHER" {
                        return ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(
                            Self(SslFailures::OTHER.bits()),
                        );
                    }
                };
                let _ = name;
                ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::None
            }
            /// Whether all bits in this flags value are unset.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
                self.0 == <u32 as ::bitflags::Bits>::EMPTY
            }
            /// Whether all known bits in this flags value are set.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn is_all(&self) -> bool {
                Self::all().0 | self.0 == self.0
            }
            /// Whether any set bits in a source flags value are also set in a target flags value.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn intersects(&self, other: Self) -> bool {
                self.0 & other.0 != <u32 as ::bitflags::Bits>::EMPTY
            }
            /// Whether all set bits in a source flags value are also set in a target flags value.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn contains(&self, other: Self) -> bool {
                self.0 & other.0 == other.0
            }
            /// The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            pub fn insert(&mut self, other: Self) {
                *self = Self(self.0).union(other);
            }
            /// The intersection of a source flags value with the complement of a target flags
            /// value (`&!`).
            ///
            /// This method is not equivalent to `self & !other` when `other` has unknown bits set.
            /// `remove` won't truncate `other`, but the `!` operator will.
            #[inline]
            pub fn remove(&mut self, other: Self) {
                *self = Self(self.0).difference(other);
            }
            /// The bitwise exclusive-or (`^`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            pub fn toggle(&mut self, other: Self) {
                *self = Self(self.0).symmetric_difference(other);
            }
            /// Call `insert` when `value` is `true` or `remove` when `value` is `false`.
            #[inline]
            pub fn set(&mut self, other: Self, value: bool) {
                if value {
                    self.insert(other);
                } else {
                    self.remove(other);
                }
            }
            /// The bitwise and (`&`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn intersection(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                Self(self.0 & other.0)
            }
            /// The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn union(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                Self(self.0 | other.0)
            }
            /// The intersection of a source flags value with the complement of a target flags
            /// value (`&!`).
            ///
            /// This method is not equivalent to `self & !other` when `other` has unknown bits set.
            /// `difference` won't truncate `other`, but the `!` operator will.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn difference(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                Self(self.0 & !other.0)
            }
            /// The bitwise exclusive-or (`^`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn symmetric_difference(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                Self(self.0 ^ other.0)
            }
            /// The bitwise negation (`!`) of the bits in a flags value, truncating the result.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn complement(self) -> Self {
                Self::from_bits_truncate(!self.0)
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::fmt::Binary for InternalBitFlags {
            fn fmt(
                &self,
                f: &mut ::bitflags::__private::core::fmt::Formatter,

                let inner = self.0;
                ::bitflags::__private::core::fmt::Binary::fmt(&inner, f)

        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::ops::BitOr for InternalBitFlags {
            type Output = Self;
            /// The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            fn bitor(self, other: InternalBitFlags) -> Self {
                self.union(other)
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::ops::BitOrAssign for InternalBitFlags {
            /// The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            fn bitor_assign(&mut self, other: Self) {
                self.insert(other);
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::ops::BitXor for InternalBitFlags {
            type Output = Self;
            /// The bitwise exclusive-or (`^`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            fn bitxor(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                self.symmetric_difference(other)
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::ops::BitXorAssign for InternalBitFlags {
            /// The bitwise exclusive-or (`^`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            fn bitxor_assign(&mut self, other: Self) {
                self.toggle(other);
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::ops::BitAnd for InternalBitFlags {
            type Output = Self;
            /// The bitwise and (`&`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            fn bitand(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                self.intersection(other)
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::ops::BitAndAssign for InternalBitFlags {
            /// The bitwise and (`&`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            fn bitand_assign(&mut self, other: Self) {
                *self = Self::from_bits_retain(self.bits()).intersection(other);
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::ops::Sub for InternalBitFlags {
            type Output = Self;
            /// The intersection of a source flags value with the complement of a target flags value (`&!`).
            ///
            /// This method is not equivalent to `self & !other` when `other` has unknown bits set.
            /// `difference` won't truncate `other`, but the `!` operator will.
            #[inline]
            fn sub(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                self.difference(other)
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::ops::SubAssign for InternalBitFlags {
            /// The intersection of a source flags value with the complement of a target flags value (`&!`).
            ///
            /// This method is not equivalent to `self & !other` when `other` has unknown bits set.
            /// `difference` won't truncate `other`, but the `!` operator will.
            #[inline]
            fn sub_assign(&mut self, other: Self) {
                self.remove(other);
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::ops::Not for InternalBitFlags {
            type Output = Self;
            /// The bitwise negation (`!`) of the bits in a flags value, truncating the result.
            #[inline]
            fn not(self) -> Self {
                self.complement()
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::iter::Extend<InternalBitFlags>
        for InternalBitFlags {
            /// The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in each flags value.
            fn extend<T: ::bitflags::__private::core::iter::IntoIterator<Item = Self>>(
                &mut self,
                iterator: T,
            ) {
                for item in iterator {
                    self.insert(item)
                }
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::iter::FromIterator<InternalBitFlags>
        for InternalBitFlags {
            /// The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in each flags value.
            fn from_iter<
                T: ::bitflags::__private::core::iter::IntoIterator<Item = Self>,
            >(iterator: T) -> Self {
                use ::bitflags::__private::core::iter::Extend;
                let mut result = Self::empty();
                result.extend(iterator);
                result
            }
        }
        impl InternalBitFlags {
            /// Yield a set of contained flags values.
            ///
            /// Each yielded flags value will correspond to a defined named flag. Any unknown bits
            /// will be yielded together as a final flags value.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn iter(&self) -> ::bitflags::iter::Iter<SslFailures> {
                ::bitflags::iter::Iter::__private_const_new(
                    <SslFailures as ::bitflags::Flags>::FLAGS,
                    SslFailures::from_bits_retain(self.bits()),
                    SslFailures::from_bits_retain(self.bits()),
                )
            }
            /// Yield a set of contained named flags values.
            ///
            /// This method is like [`iter`](#method.iter), except only yields bits in contained named flags.
            /// Any unknown bits, or bits not corresponding to a contained flag will not be yielded.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn iter_names(&self) -> ::bitflags::iter::IterNames<SslFailures> {
                ::bitflags::iter::IterNames::__private_const_new(
                    <SslFailures as ::bitflags::Flags>::FLAGS,
                    SslFailures::from_bits_retain(self.bits()),
                    SslFailures::from_bits_retain(self.bits()),
                )
            }
        }
        impl ::bitflags::__private::core::iter::IntoIterator for InternalBitFlags {
            type Item = SslFailures;
            type IntoIter = ::bitflags::iter::Iter<SslFailures>;
            fn into_iter(self) -> Self::IntoIter {
                self.iter()
            }
        }
        impl InternalBitFlags {
            /// Returns a mutable reference to the raw value of the flags currently stored.
            #[inline]
            pub fn bits_mut(&mut self) -> &mut u32 {
                &mut self.0
            }
        }
        #[allow(dead_code, deprecated, unused_attributes)]
        impl SslFailures {
            /// Get a flags value with all bits unset.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn empty() -> Self {
                Self(InternalBitFlags::empty())
            }
            /// Get a flags value with all known bits set.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn all() -> Self {
                Self(InternalBitFlags::all())
            }
            /// Get the underlying bits value.
            ///
            /// The returned value is exactly the bits set in this flags value.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn bits(&self) -> u32 {
                self.0.bits()
            }
            /// Convert from a bits value.
            ///
            /// This method will return `None` if any unknown bits are set.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn from_bits(
                bits: u32,
            ) -> ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option<Self> {
                match InternalBitFlags::from_bits(bits) {
                    ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(bits) => {
                        ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(Self(bits))
                    }
                    ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::None => {
                        ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::None
                    }
                }
            }
            /// Convert from a bits value, unsetting any unknown bits.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn from_bits_truncate(bits: u32) -> Self {
                Self(InternalBitFlags::from_bits_truncate(bits))
            }
            /// Convert from a bits value exactly.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn from_bits_retain(bits: u32) -> Self {
                Self(InternalBitFlags::from_bits_retain(bits))
            }
            /// Get a flags value with the bits of a flag with the given name set.
            ///
            /// This method will return `None` if `name` is empty or doesn't
            /// correspond to any named flag.
            #[inline]
            pub fn from_name(
                name: &str,
            ) -> ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option<Self> {
                match InternalBitFlags::from_name(name) {
                    ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(bits) => {
                        ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::Some(Self(bits))
                    }
                    ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::None => {
                        ::bitflags::__private::core::option::Option::None
                    }
                }
            }
            /// Whether all bits in this flags value are unset.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
                self.0.is_empty()
            }
            /// Whether all known bits in this flags value are set.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn is_all(&self) -> bool {
                self.0.is_all()
            }
            /// Whether any set bits in a source flags value are also set in a target flags value.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn intersects(&self, other: Self) -> bool {
                self.0.intersects(other.0)
            }
            /// Whether all set bits in a source flags value are also set in a target flags value.
            #[inline]
            pub const fn contains(&self, other: Self) -> bool {
                self.0.contains(other.0)
            }
            /// The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            pub fn insert(&mut self, other: Self) {
                self.0.insert(other.0)
            }
            /// The intersection of a source flags value with the complement of a target flags
            /// value (`&!`).
            ///
            /// This method is not equivalent to `self & !other` when `other` has unknown bits set.
            /// `remove` won't truncate `other`, but the `!` operator will.
            #[inline]
            pub fn remove(&mut self, other: Self) {
                self.0.remove(other.0)
            }
            /// The bitwise exclusive-or (`^`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            pub fn toggle(&mut self, other: Self) {
                self.0.toggle(other.0)
            }
            /// Call `insert` when `value` is `true` or `remove` when `value` is `false`.
            #[inline]
            pub fn set(&mut self, other: Self, value: bool) {
                self.0.set(other.0, value)
            }
            /// The bitwise and (`&`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn intersection(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                Self(self.0.intersection(other.0))
            }
            /// The bitwise or (`|`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn union(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                Self(self.0.union(other.0))
            }
            /// The intersection of a source flags value with the complement of a target flags
            /// value (`&!`).
            ///
            /// This method is not equivalent to `self & !other` when `other` has unknown bits set.
            /// `difference` won't truncate `other`, but the `!` operator will.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn difference(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                Self(self.0.difference(other.0))
            }
            /// The bitwise exclusive-or (`^`) of the bits in two flags values.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn symmetric_difference(self, other: Self) -> Self {
                Self(self.0.symmetric_difference(other.0))
            }
            /// The bitwise negation (`!`) of the bits in a flags value, truncating the result.
            #[inline]
            #[must_use]
            pub const fn complement(self) -> Self {
                Self(self.0.complement())
            }
        }

    impl SslServerCertInfo {
        unsafe fn from_raw(info: *const ffi::svn_auth_ssl_server_cert_info_t) -> Self {
            let info = unsafe { info.as_ref().unwrap() };
            unsafe {
                let hostname = apr::char_array_to_string(info.hostname).unwrap();
                let fingerprint = apr::char_array_to_string(info.fingerprint).unwrap();
                let valid_from = apr::char_array_to_string(info.valid_from).unwrap();
                let valid_until = apr::char_array_to_string(info.valid_until).unwrap();
                let issuer = apr::char_array_to_string(info.issuer_dname).unwrap();
                let ascii_cert = apr::char_array_to_string(info.ascii_cert).unwrap();
                Self {
                    hostname,
                    fingerprint,
                    valid_from,
                    valid_until,
                    issuer,
                    ascii_cert,
                }
            }
        }
    }
    pub struct TrustServer {
        accept_failures: u32,
        save: bool,
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    impl ::core::fmt::Debug for TrustServer {
        #[inline]
        fn fmt(&self, f: &mut ::core::fmt::Formatter) -> ::core::fmt::Result {
            ::core::fmt::Formatter::debug_struct_field2_finish(
                f,
                "TrustServer",
                "accept_failures",
                &self.accept_failures,
                "save",
                &&self.save,
            )
        }
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    impl ::core::clone::Clone for TrustServer {
        #[inline]
        fn clone(&self) -> TrustServer {
            let _: ::core::clone::AssertParamIsClone<u32>;
            let _: ::core::clone::AssertParamIsClone<bool>;
            *self
        }
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    impl ::core::marker::Copy for TrustServer {}
    impl TrustServer {
        ///Constructs a new `TrustServer`.
        pub fn new(accept_failures: u32, save: bool) -> Self {
            TrustServer {
                accept_failures: accept_failures,
                save: save,
            }
        }
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    impl ::core::default::Default for TrustServer {
        #[inline]
        fn default() -> TrustServer {
            TrustServer {
                accept_failures: ::core::default::Default::default(),
                save: ::core::default::Default::default(),
            }
        }
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    unsafe impl<UT> ::uniffi::FfiConverter<UT> for TrustServer {
        type FfiType = ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer;
        fn lower(v: Self) -> ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer {
            let mut buf = ::std::vec::Vec::new();
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(v, &mut buf);
            ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer::from_vec(buf)
        }
        fn try_lift(buf: ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer) -> ::uniffi_core::Result<Self> {
            let vec = buf.destroy_into_vec();
            let mut buf = vec.as_slice();
            let value = <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::try_read(&mut buf)?;
            match ::uniffi_core::deps::bytes::Buf::remaining(&buf) {
                0 => ::std::result::Result::Ok(value),
                n => {
                    return ::anyhow::__private::Err({
                        let error = ::anyhow::__private::format_err(
                            format_args!(
                                "junk data left in buffer after lifting (count: {0})",
                                n,
                            ),
                        );
                        error
                    });
                }
            }
        }
        fn write(obj: Self, buf: &mut ::std::vec::Vec<u8>) {
            <u32 as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.accept_failures, buf);
            <bool as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.save, buf);
        }
        fn try_read(
            buf: &mut &[::std::primitive::u8],
        ) -> ::uniffi::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            ::std::result::Result::Ok(Self {
                accept_failures: <u32 as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                save: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
            })
        }
        const TYPE_ID_META: ::uniffi::MetadataBuffer = ::uniffi::MetadataBuffer::from_code(
                ::uniffi::metadata::codes::TYPE_RECORD,
            )
            .concat_str("engine")
            .concat_str("TrustServer");
    }
    unsafe impl<UT> ::uniffi_core::Lower<UT> for TrustServer {
        type FfiType = <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<UT>>::FfiType;
        fn lower(obj: Self) -> Self::FfiType {
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<UT>>::lower(obj)
        }
        fn write(obj: Self, buf: &mut ::std::vec::Vec<u8>) {
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<UT>>::write(obj, buf)
        }
    }
    unsafe impl<UT> ::uniffi_core::Lift<UT> for TrustServer {
        type FfiType = <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<UT>>::FfiType;
        fn try_lift(v: Self::FfiType) -> ::uniffi_core::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<UT>>::try_lift(v)
        }
        fn try_read(buf: &mut &[u8]) -> ::uniffi_core::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<UT>>::try_read(buf)
        }
    }
    unsafe impl<UT> ::uniffi_core::LowerReturn<UT> for TrustServer {
        type ReturnType = <Self as ::uniffi_core::Lower<UT>>::FfiType;

        type LiftType = Self;

    impl<UT> ::uniffi_core::ConvertError<UT> for TrustServer {
        fn try_convert_unexpected_callback_error(
            e: ::uniffi_core::UnexpectedUniFFICallbackError,
        ) -> ::uniffi_core::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {

            )
        }

                save: ::core::clone::Clone::clone(&self.save),

            }

    unsafe impl<UT> ::uniffi::FfiConverter<UT> for Authentication {
        type FfiType = ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer;
        fn lower(v: Self) -> ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer {
            let mut buf = ::std::vec::Vec::new();
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(v, &mut buf);
            ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer::from_vec(buf)
        }
        fn try_lift(buf: ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer) -> ::uniffi_core::Result<Self> {
            let vec = buf.destroy_into_vec();
            let mut buf = vec.as_slice();
            let value = <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::try_read(&mut buf)?;
            match ::uniffi_core::deps::bytes::Buf::remaining(&buf) {
                0 => ::std::result::Result::Ok(value),
                n => {
                    return ::anyhow::__private::Err({
                        let error = ::anyhow::__private::format_err(
                            format_args!(
                                "junk data left in buffer after lifting (count: {0})",
                                n,
                            ),
                        );
                        error
                    });
                }
            }
        }
        fn write(obj: Self, buf: &mut ::std::vec::Vec<u8>) {
            <String as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.username, buf);
            <String as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.password, buf);
            <bool as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.save, buf);
        }
        fn try_read(
            buf: &mut &[::std::primitive::u8],
        ) -> ::uniffi::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            ::std::result::Result::Ok(Self {
                username: <String as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                password: <String as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                save: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
            })
        }

                ::uniffi::metadata::codes::TYPE_RECORD,
            )
            .concat_str("engine")
            .concat_str("Authentication");
    }

    unsafe extern "C" fn ssl_server_trust_prompt(
        cred: *mut *mut ffi::svn_auth_cred_ssl_server_trust_t,
        baton: *mut c_void,
        realm: *const c_char,
        failures: ffi::apr_uint32_t,
        info: *const ffi::svn_auth_ssl_server_cert_info_t,
        may_save: ffi::svn_boolean_t,
        pool: *mut ffi::apr_pool_t,
    ) -> *mut ffi::svn_error_t {
        unsafe {
            let realm = CStr::from_ptr(realm).to_str().unwrap();
            let info = SslServerCertInfo::from_raw(info);
            {
                use ::tracing::__macro_support::Callsite as _;
                static __CALLSITE: ::tracing::callsite::DefaultCallsite = {
                    static META: ::tracing::Metadata<'static> = {
                        ::tracing_core::metadata::Metadata::new(
                            "event src/subversion/context.rs:2268",
                            "engine::subversion::context",
                            ::tracing::Level::INFO,
                            ::tracing_core::__macro_support::Option::Some(
                                "src/subversion/context.rs",
                            ),
                            ::tracing_core::__macro_support::Option::Some(2268u32),
                            ::tracing_core::__macro_support::Option::Some(
                                "engine::subversion::context",
                            ),
                            ::tracing_core::field::FieldSet::new(
                                &["message"],
                                ::tracing_core::callsite::Identifier(&__CALLSITE),
                            ),
                            ::tracing::metadata::Kind::EVENT,
                        )
                    };
                    ::tracing::callsite::DefaultCallsite::new(&META)
                };
                let enabled = ::tracing::Level::INFO
                    <= ::tracing::level_filters::STATIC_MAX_LEVEL
                    && ::tracing::Level::INFO
                        <= ::tracing::level_filters::LevelFilter::current()
                    && {
                        let interest = __CALLSITE.interest();
                        !interest.is_never()
                            && ::tracing::__macro_support::__is_enabled(
                                __CALLSITE.metadata(),
                                interest,
                            )
                    };
                if enabled {
                    (|value_set: ::tracing::field::ValueSet| {
                        let meta = __CALLSITE.metadata();
                        ::tracing::Event::dispatch(meta, &value_set);
                    })({
                        #[allow(unused_imports)]
                        use ::tracing::field::{debug, display, Value};
                        let mut iter = __CALLSITE.metadata().fields().iter();
                        __CALLSITE
                            .metadata()
                            .fields()
                            .value_set(
                                &[
                                    (
                                        &::tracing::__macro_support::Iterator::next(&mut iter)
                                            .expect("FieldSet corrupted (this is a bug)"),
                                        ::tracing::__macro_support::Option::Some(
                                            &format_args!(
                                                "Server info: realm={0} failures={1} info={2:?} may_save={3}",
                                                realm,
                                                failures,
                                                info,
                                                may_save,
                                            ) as &dyn Value,
                                        ),
                                    ),
                                ],
                            )
                    });
                } else {
                }
            };
            let this = &mut *(baton as *mut ContextInner);
            let trust = this
                .context_notifier
                .ssl_server_trust_prompt(
                    realm.to_string(),
                    failures,
                    info,
                    may_save != 0,
                );
            match trust {
                Some(trust) => {
                    let mut pool = ManuallyDrop::new(apr::Pool::from_raw(pool));
                    *cred = pool.malloc::<ffi::svn_auth_cred_ssl_server_trust_t>();
                    let cred = &mut **cred;
                    cred.accepted_failures = trust.accept_failures;
                    cred.may_save = trust.save as _;
                }
                None => {
                    *cred = std::ptr::null_mut();
                }
            }
            svn_no_error()
        }
    }
    enum LogChangedPathAction {
        Add,
        Delete,
        Replace,
        Modify,
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    unsafe impl<UT> ::uniffi::FfiConverter<UT> for LogChangedPathAction {
        type FfiType = ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer;
        fn lower(v: Self) -> ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer {
            let mut buf = ::std::vec::Vec::new();
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(v, &mut buf);
            ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer::from_vec(buf)
        }
        fn try_lift(buf: ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer) -> ::uniffi_core::Result<Self> {
            let vec = buf.destroy_into_vec();
            let mut buf = vec.as_slice();
            let value = <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::try_read(&mut buf)?;
            match ::uniffi_core::deps::bytes::Buf::remaining(&buf) {
                0 => ::std::result::Result::Ok(value),
                n => {
                    return ::anyhow::__private::Err({
                        let error = ::anyhow::__private::format_err(
                            format_args!(
                                "junk data left in buffer after lifting (count: {0})",
                                n,
                            ),
                        );
                        error
                    });
                }
            }
        }
        fn write(obj: Self, buf: &mut ::std::vec::Vec<u8>) {
            match obj {
                Self::Add {} => {
                    ::uniffi::deps::bytes::BufMut::put_i32(buf, 1);
                }
                Self::Delete {} => {
                    ::uniffi::deps::bytes::BufMut::put_i32(buf, 2);
                }
                Self::Replace {} => {
                    ::uniffi::deps::bytes::BufMut::put_i32(buf, 3);
                }
                Self::Modify {} => {
                    ::uniffi::deps::bytes::BufMut::put_i32(buf, 4);
                }
            }
        }
        fn try_read(
            buf: &mut &[::std::primitive::u8],
        ) -> ::uniffi::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            ::uniffi::check_remaining(buf, 4)?;
            ::std::result::Result::Ok(
                match ::uniffi::deps::bytes::Buf::get_i32(buf) {
                    1 => Self::Add {},
                    2 => Self::Delete {},
                    3 => Self::Replace {},
                    4 => Self::Modify {},
                    v => {
                        return ::anyhow::__private::Err(
                            ::anyhow::Error::msg(
                                ::alloc::__export::must_use({
                                    ::alloc::fmt::format(
                                        format_args!(
                                            "Invalid LogChangedPathAction enum value: {0}",
                                            v,
                                        ),
                                    )
                                }),
                            ),
                        );
                    }
                },
            )
        }
        const TYPE_ID_META: ::uniffi::MetadataBuffer = ::uniffi::MetadataBuffer::from_code(
                ::uniffi::metadata::codes::TYPE_ENUM,
            )
            .concat_str("engine")
            .concat_str("LogChangedPathAction");
    }

            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<UT>>::lower(obj)

    impl ::core::fmt::Debug for LogChangedPathAction {
        #[inline]
        fn fmt(&self, f: &mut ::core::fmt::Formatter) -> ::core::fmt::Result {
            ::core::fmt::Formatter::write_str(
                f,
                match self {
                    LogChangedPathAction::Add => "Add",
                    LogChangedPathAction::Delete => "Delete",

                    LogChangedPathAction::Modify => "Modify",
                },

    impl From<LogChangedPathAction> for u8 {
        fn from(value: LogChangedPathAction) -> Self {
            match value {
                LogChangedPathAction::Add => b'A',
                LogChangedPathAction::Delete => b'D',
                LogChangedPathAction::Replace => b'R',
                LogChangedPathAction::Modify => b'M',
            }
        }
    }
    impl TryFrom<u8> for LogChangedPathAction {
        type Error = ();
        fn try_from(value: u8) -> Result<Self, Self::Error> {
            match value {
                b'A' => Ok(LogChangedPathAction::Add),
                b'D' => Ok(LogChangedPathAction::Delete),
                b'R' => Ok(LogChangedPathAction::Replace),
                b'M' => Ok(LogChangedPathAction::Modify),
                _ => Err(()),
            }
        }
    }

        fn from_i8(value: i8) -> Self {
            Self::try_from(u8::try_from(value).unwrap()).unwrap()
        }

    pub struct LogChangedPathEntry {
        action: LogChangedPathAction,
        copy_from_path: String,
        copy_from_revision: RevisionNumber,
        node_kind: NodeKind,
        text_modified: Option<bool>,
        props_modified: Option<bool>,
    }
    impl LogChangedPathEntry {
        ///Constructs a new `LogChangedPathEntry`.
        pub fn new(
            action: LogChangedPathAction,
            copy_from_path: String,
            copy_from_revision: RevisionNumber,
            node_kind: NodeKind,
            text_modified: Option<bool>,
            props_modified: Option<bool>,
        ) -> Self {
            LogChangedPathEntry {
                action: action,
                copy_from_path: copy_from_path,
                copy_from_revision: copy_from_revision,
                node_kind: node_kind,
                text_modified: text_modified,
                props_modified: props_modified,
            }
        }
    }

    unsafe impl<UT> ::uniffi::FfiConverter<UT> for LogChangedPathEntry {
        type FfiType = ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer;
        fn lower(v: Self) -> ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer {
            let mut buf = ::std::vec::Vec::new();
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(v, &mut buf);
            ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer::from_vec(buf)
        }
        fn try_lift(buf: ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer) -> ::uniffi_core::Result<Self> {
            let vec = buf.destroy_into_vec();
            let mut buf = vec.as_slice();
            let value = <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::try_read(&mut buf)?;
            match ::uniffi_core::deps::bytes::Buf::remaining(&buf) {
                0 => ::std::result::Result::Ok(value),
                n => {
                    return ::anyhow::__private::Err({
                        let error = ::anyhow::__private::format_err(
                            format_args!(
                                "junk data left in buffer after lifting (count: {0})",
                                n,
                            ),
                        );
                        error
                    });
                }
            }
        }
        fn write(obj: Self, buf: &mut ::std::vec::Vec<u8>) {
            <LogChangedPathAction as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.action, buf);
            <String as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.copy_from_path, buf);
            <RevisionNumber as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.copy_from_revision, buf);
            <NodeKind as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.node_kind, buf);
            <Option<
                bool,
            > as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.text_modified, buf);
            <Option<
                bool,
            > as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.props_modified, buf);
        }
        fn try_read(
            buf: &mut &[::std::primitive::u8],
        ) -> ::uniffi::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            ::std::result::Result::Ok(Self {
                action: <LogChangedPathAction as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                copy_from_path: <String as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                copy_from_revision: <RevisionNumber as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                node_kind: <NodeKind as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                text_modified: <Option<
                    bool,
                > as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                props_modified: <Option<
                    bool,
                > as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
            })
        }

    #[unsafe(no_mangle)]

    pub static UNIFFI_META_ENGINE_RECORD_LOGCHANGEDPATHENTRY: [u8; UNIFFI_META_CONST_ENGINE_RECORD_LOGCHANGEDPATHENTRY
        .size] = UNIFFI_META_CONST_ENGINE_RECORD_LOGCHANGEDPATHENTRY.into_array();
    fn from_svn_stristate(value: u32) -> Option<bool> {
        match value {
            ffi::svn_tristate_t_svn_tristate_true => Some(true),
            ffi::svn_tristate_t_svn_tristate_false => Some(false),
            ffi::svn_tristate_t_svn_tristate_unknown => None,
            _ => {
                ::core::panicking::panic_fmt(
                    format_args!("Invalid svn tristate value: {0}", value),
                );
            }
        }
    }
    impl LogChangedPathEntry {
        unsafe fn from_raw(ptr: *const ffi::svn_log_changed_path2_t) -> Self {
            unsafe {
                let ptr = &*ptr;
                let action = LogChangedPathAction::from_i8(ptr.action);
                let copy_from_path = apr::char_array_to_string(ptr.copyfrom_path)
                    .unwrap();
                let copy_from_revision = ptr.copyfrom_rev.try_into().unwrap();
                let node_kind = NodeKind::try_from(ptr.node_kind).unwrap();
                let text_modified = from_svn_stristate(ptr.text_modified);
                let props_modified = from_svn_stristate(ptr.props_modified);
                Self {
                    action,
                    copy_from_path,
                    copy_from_revision,
                    node_kind,
                    text_modified,
                    props_modified,
                }
            }
        }
    }
    pub struct LogEntry {
        revision: RevisionNumber,
        date: Option<i64>,
        author: Option<String>,
        message: Option<String>,
        changed_path_entries: HashMap<String, LogChangedPathEntry>,
        has_children: bool,
        non_inheritable: bool,
        subtractive_merge: bool,
    }

                changed_path_entries: ::core::default::Default::default(),

    }

            has_children: bool,

                changed_path_entries: changed_path_entries,
                has_children: has_children,
                non_inheritable: non_inheritable,
                subtractive_merge: subtractive_merge,

        }
    }

    #[unsafe(no_mangle)]
    #[doc(hidden)]
    pub static UNIFFI_META_ENGINE_RECORD_LOGENTRY: [u8; UNIFFI_META_CONST_ENGINE_RECORD_LOGENTRY
        .size] = UNIFFI_META_CONST_ENGINE_RECORD_LOGENTRY.into_array();
    impl LogEntry {
        fn from_raw(ptr: *mut ffi::svn_log_entry_t) -> Self {
            unsafe {
                let log_entry = &*ptr;
                let mut author: *const c_char = std::ptr::null();
                let mut date: *const c_char = std::ptr::null();
                let mut message: *const c_char = std::ptr::null();
                ffi::svn_compat_log_revprops_out(
                    &mut author as _,
                    &mut date as _,
                    &mut message as _,
                    log_entry.revprops,
                );
                let revision = log_entry.revision.try_into().unwrap();
                let mut pool = apr::Pool::create();
                let mut time_is_valid = false;
                let mut time: ffi::apr_time_t = 0;
                if !date.is_null() {
                    let error = ffi::svn_time_from_cstring(
                        &mut time as *mut _,
                        date,
                        pool.as_mut_ptr(),
                    );
                    if error.is_null() {
                        time_is_valid = true;
                    } else {
                        ffi::svn_error_clear(error);
                    }
                }
                let author = apr::char_array_to_string(author);
                let message = apr::char_array_to_string(message);
                let date = if time_is_valid { Some(time) } else { None };
                let mut changed_path_entries = HashMap::new();
                if !log_entry.changed_paths2.is_null() {
                    let mut it = ffi::apr_hash_first(
                        pool.as_mut_ptr(),
                        log_entry.changed_paths2,
                    );
                    while !it.is_null() {
                        let mut key = std::ptr::null();
                        let mut value = std::ptr::null_mut();
                        ffi::apr_hash_this(
                            it,
                            &mut key as *mut _,
                            std::ptr::null_mut(),
                            &mut value as *mut _,
                        );
                        let path = apr::char_array_to_string(key as _).unwrap();
                        let log_changed_path_entry = LogChangedPathEntry::from_raw(
                            value as _,
                        );
                        changed_path_entries.insert(path, log_changed_path_entry);
                        it = ffi::apr_hash_next(it);
                    }
                }
                let has_children = log_entry.has_children != 0;
                let non_inheritable = log_entry.non_inheritable != 0;
                let subtractive_merge = log_entry.subtractive_merge != 0;
                Self {
                    revision,
                    date,
                    author,
                    message,
                    changed_path_entries,
                    has_children,
                    non_inheritable,
                    subtractive_merge,
                }
            }
        }
    }
    unsafe extern "C" fn on_import_filter(
        baton: *mut c_void,
        filtered: *mut ffi::svn_boolean_t,
        local_abspath: *const c_char,
        direct: *const ffi::svn_io_dirent2_t,
        pool: *mut ffi::apr_pool_t,
    ) -> *mut ffi::svn_error_t {
        svn_no_error()
    }
    unsafe extern "C" fn on_receive_log_entry(
        baton: *mut c_void,
        log_entry: *mut ffi::svn_log_entry_t,
        pool: *mut ffi::apr_pool_t,
    ) -> *mut ffi::svn_error_t {
        unsafe {
            let error = on_cancel(baton);
            if !error.is_null() {
                return error;
            }
            let this = &mut *(baton as *mut ContextInner);
            this.log_entries.push(LogEntry::from_raw(log_entry));
        }
        svn_no_error()
    }
    unsafe extern "C" fn on_authenticate(
        cred: *mut *mut ffi::svn_auth_cred_simple_t,
        baton: *mut c_void,
        realm: *const c_char,
        username: *const c_char,
        may_save: ffi::svn_boolean_t,
        pool: *mut ffi::apr_pool_t,
    ) -> *mut ffi::svn_error_t {
        unsafe {
            let cancel = on_cancel(baton);
            if !cancel.is_null() {
                return cancel;
            }
            let context = (baton as *mut ContextInner).as_mut().unwrap();
            if let Some(result) = context
                .context_notifier
                .authenticate(
                    char_array_to_string(realm).unwrap(),
                    char_array_to_string(username).unwrap(),
                    may_save != 0,
                )
            {
                let mut pool = ManuallyDrop::new(apr::Pool::from_raw(pool));
                *cred = pool.malloc();
                let cred = (*cred).as_mut().unwrap();
                cred.may_save = result.save.into();
                cred.password = pool.string(result.password).unwrap();
                cred.username = pool.string(result.username).unwrap();
                svn_no_error()
            } else {
                on_cancel(baton)
            }
        }
    }
    pub struct ContextFactory;
    impl Drop for ContextFactory {
        fn drop(&mut self) {
            unsafe {
                ffi::apr_terminate2();
            }
        }
    }
    impl ContextFactory {
        pub fn create_context(
            &self,
            opts: CreateContextOptions,
        ) -> error::Result<Context> {
            Context::create(opts)
        }
        pub fn instance() -> error::Result<&'static ContextFactory> {
            static SELF: once_cell::sync::OnceCell<ContextFactory> = once_cell::sync::OnceCell::new();
            SELF.get_or_try_init(|| unsafe {
                apr::initialize()?;
                let error = ffi::svn_dso_initialize2();
                SVNError::from_nullable_ptr(error).context(builder::Svn)?;
                let pool = ffi::svn_pool_create_ex(
                    std::ptr::null_mut(),
                    std::ptr::null_mut(),
                );
                ffi::svn_utf_initialize2(0, pool);
                let error = ffi::svn_nls_init();
                SVNError::from_nullable_ptr(error).context(builder::Svn)?;
                Ok(ContextFactory {})
            })
        }
    }

                &&self.added_keep_local,
            ];

                f,

                names,
                values,
            )
        }
    }

        type FfiType = ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer;

            let mut buf = ::std::vec::Vec::new();
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(v, &mut buf);
            ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer::from_vec(buf)
        }
        fn try_lift(buf: ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer) -> ::uniffi_core::Result<Self> {
            let vec = buf.destroy_into_vec();
            let mut buf = vec.as_slice();
            let value = <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::try_read(&mut buf)?;
            match ::uniffi_core::deps::bytes::Buf::remaining(&buf) {
                0 => ::std::result::Result::Ok(value),
                n => {
                    return ::anyhow::__private::Err({
                        let error = ::anyhow::__private::format_err(
                            format_args!(
                                "junk data left in buffer after lifting (count: {0})",
                                n,
                            ),
                        );
                        error
                    });
                }
            }
        }

            <Vec<String> as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.paths, buf);
            <Depth as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.depth, buf);
            <Vec<
                String,
            > as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.changelists, buf);
            <bool as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.clear_changelists, buf);
            <bool as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.metadata_only, buf);
            <bool as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.added_keep_local, buf);
        }
        fn try_read(
            buf: &mut &[::std::primitive::u8],
        ) -> ::uniffi::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            ::std::result::Result::Ok(Self {
                paths: <Vec<String> as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                depth: <Depth as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                changelists: <Vec<
                    String,
                > as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                clear_changelists: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                metadata_only: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                added_keep_local: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
            })
        }

                ::uniffi::metadata::codes::TYPE_RECORD,
            )
            .concat_str("engine")
            .concat_str("RevertOptions");
    }

                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.peg_revision, buf);
            <u32 as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.limit, buf);
            <Vec<
                RevisionRange,
            > as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.revsions, buf);
            <bool as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.discover_changed_paths, buf);
            <bool as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.strict_node_history, buf);
            <bool as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.include_merged_revisions, buf);
            <Vec<
                String,
            > as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.revisions_properties, buf);
        }
        fn try_read(
            buf: &mut &[::std::primitive::u8],
        ) -> ::uniffi::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            ::std::result::Result::Ok(Self {
                targets: <Vec<
                    String,
                > as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                peg_revision: <Revision as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                limit: <u32 as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                revsions: <Vec<
                    RevisionRange,
                > as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                discover_changed_paths: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                strict_node_history: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                include_merged_revisions: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                revisions_properties: <Vec<
                    String,
                > as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
            })
        }

    pub struct ImportResult {
        items: Vec<CommitItem>,
        info: CommitInfo,
    }
    impl ImportResult {
        ///Constructs a new `ImportResult`.
        pub fn new(items: Vec<CommitItem>, info: CommitInfo) -> Self {
            ImportResult {
                items: items,
                info: info,
            }
        }
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    impl ::core::fmt::Debug for ImportResult {
        #[inline]
        fn fmt(&self, f: &mut ::core::fmt::Formatter) -> ::core::fmt::Result {
            ::core::fmt::Formatter::debug_struct_field2_finish(
                f,
                "ImportResult",
                "items",
                &self.items,
                "info",
                &&self.info,
            )
        }
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    unsafe impl<UT> ::uniffi::FfiConverter<UT> for ImportResult {
        type FfiType = ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer;
        fn lower(v: Self) -> ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer {
            let mut buf = ::std::vec::Vec::new();
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(v, &mut buf);
            ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer::from_vec(buf)
        }
        fn try_lift(buf: ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer) -> ::uniffi_core::Result<Self> {
            let vec = buf.destroy_into_vec();
            let mut buf = vec.as_slice();
            let value = <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::try_read(&mut buf)?;
            match ::uniffi_core::deps::bytes::Buf::remaining(&buf) {
                0 => ::std::result::Result::Ok(value),
                n => {
                    return ::anyhow::__private::Err({
                        let error = ::anyhow::__private::format_err(
                            format_args!(
                                "junk data left in buffer after lifting (count: {0})",
                                n,
                            ),
                        );
                        error
                    });
                }
            }
        }
        fn write(obj: Self, buf: &mut ::std::vec::Vec<u8>) {
            <Vec<
                CommitItem,
            > as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.items, buf);
            <CommitInfo as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.info, buf);
        }
        fn try_read(
            buf: &mut &[::std::primitive::u8],
        ) -> ::uniffi::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            ::std::result::Result::Ok(Self {
                items: <Vec<
                    CommitItem,
                > as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                info: <CommitInfo as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
            })
        }
        const TYPE_ID_META: ::uniffi::MetadataBuffer = ::uniffi::MetadataBuffer::from_code(
                ::uniffi::metadata::codes::TYPE_RECORD,
            )
            .concat_str("engine")
            .concat_str("ImportResult");
    }

    pub struct ImportOptions {
        path: String,
        url: String,
        depth: Depth,
        no_ignore: bool,
        no_autoprops: bool,
        ignore_unknown_node_types: bool,
        revision_property_table: HashMap<String, String>,
        commit_message: String,
    }
    impl ImportOptions {
        ///Constructs a new `ImportOptions`.
        pub fn new(
            path: String,
            url: String,
            depth: Depth,
            no_ignore: bool,
            no_autoprops: bool,
            ignore_unknown_node_types: bool,
            revision_property_table: HashMap<String, String>,
            commit_message: String,
        ) -> Self {
            ImportOptions {
                path: path,
                url: url,
                depth: depth,
                no_ignore: no_ignore,
                no_autoprops: no_autoprops,
                ignore_unknown_node_types: ignore_unknown_node_types,
                revision_property_table: revision_property_table,
                commit_message: commit_message,
            }
        }
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    impl ::core::fmt::Debug for ImportOptions {
        #[inline]
        fn fmt(&self, f: &mut ::core::fmt::Formatter) -> ::core::fmt::Result {
            let names: &'static _ = &[
                "path",
                "url",
                "depth",
                "no_ignore",
                "no_autoprops",
                "ignore_unknown_node_types",
                "revision_property_table",
                "commit_message",
            ];
            let values: &[&dyn ::core::fmt::Debug] = &[
                &self.path,
                &self.url,
                &self.depth,
                &self.no_ignore,
                &self.no_autoprops,
                &self.ignore_unknown_node_types,
                &self.revision_property_table,
                &&self.commit_message,
            ];
            ::core::fmt::Formatter::debug_struct_fields_finish(
                f,
                "ImportOptions",
                names,
                values,
            )
        }
    }

    impl apr::Pool {
        unsafe fn revision_range(
            &mut self,
            revsions: &[RevisionRange],
        ) -> *mut ffi::apr_array_header_t {
            unsafe {
                let ranges = ffi::apr_array_make(
                    self.as_mut_ptr(),
                    revsions.len().try_into().unwrap(),
                    size_of::<usize>().try_into().unwrap(),
                );
                for i in revsions {
                    let range: *mut ffi::svn_opt_revision_range_t = self.malloc();
                    let ptr = ffi::apr_array_push(ranges)
                        as *mut *mut ffi::svn_opt_revision_range_t;
                    *ptr = range;
                    let range = &mut *range;
                    range.start = i.start.to_opt_revision();
                    range.end = i.end.to_opt_revision();
                }
                ranges
            }
        }
    }

        path: String,
        peg_revision: Revision,
        revision: Revision,
        expand_keywords: bool,

    unsafe impl<UT> ::uniffi::FfiConverter<UT> for CatOptions {
        type FfiType = ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer;
        fn lower(v: Self) -> ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer {
            let mut buf = ::std::vec::Vec::new();
            <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(v, &mut buf);
            ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer::from_vec(buf)
        }
        fn try_lift(buf: ::uniffi_core::RustBuffer) -> ::uniffi_core::Result<Self> {
            let vec = buf.destroy_into_vec();
            let mut buf = vec.as_slice();
            let value = <Self as ::uniffi_core::FfiConverter<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::try_read(&mut buf)?;
            match ::uniffi_core::deps::bytes::Buf::remaining(&buf) {
                0 => ::std::result::Result::Ok(value),
                n => {
                    return ::anyhow::__private::Err({
                        let error = ::anyhow::__private::format_err(
                            format_args!(
                                "junk data left in buffer after lifting (count: {0})",
                                n,
                            ),
                        );
                        error
                    });
                }
            }
        }
        fn write(obj: Self, buf: &mut ::std::vec::Vec<u8>) {
            <String as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.path, buf);
            <Revision as ::uniffi::Lower<
                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.peg_revision, buf);
            <Revision as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.revision, buf);
            <bool as ::uniffi::Lower<crate::UniFfiTag>>::write(obj.expand_keywords, buf);
        }
        fn try_read(
            buf: &mut &[::std::primitive::u8],
        ) -> ::uniffi::deps::anyhow::Result<Self> {
            ::std::result::Result::Ok(Self {
                path: <String as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                peg_revision: <Revision as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                revision: <Revision as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                expand_keywords: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
            })
        }
        const TYPE_ID_META: ::uniffi::MetadataBuffer = ::uniffi::MetadataBuffer::from_code(
                ::uniffi::metadata::codes::TYPE_RECORD,
            )
            .concat_str("engine")
            .concat_str("CatOptions");
    }

    pub struct InfoOptions {
        path: String,
        peg_revision: Revision,
        revision: Revision,
        depth: Depth,
        fetch_excluded: bool,
        fetch_actual_only: bool,
        include_externals: bool,
        changelists: Vec<String>,
    }
    #[automatically_derived]
    impl ::core::fmt::Debug for InfoOptions {
        #[inline]
        fn fmt(&self, f: &mut ::core::fmt::Formatter) -> ::core::fmt::Result {
            let names: &'static _ = &[
                "path",
                "peg_revision",
                "revision",
                "depth",
                "fetch_excluded",
                "fetch_actual_only",
                "include_externals",
                "changelists",
            ];
            let values: &[&dyn ::core::fmt::Debug] = &[
                &self.path,
                &self.peg_revision,
                &self.revision,
                &self.depth,
                &self.fetch_excluded,
                &self.fetch_actual_only,
                &self.include_externals,
                &&self.changelists,
            ];
            ::core::fmt::Formatter::debug_struct_fields_finish(
                f,
                "InfoOptions",
                names,
                values,
            )
        }
    }

                            ),

                crate::UniFfiTag,

                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.fetch_actual_only, buf);

                crate::UniFfiTag,
            >>::write(obj.include_externals, buf);

                String,

                    crate::UniFfiTag,

                depth: <Depth as ::uniffi::Lift<crate::UniFfiTag>>::try_read(buf)?,
                fetch_excluded: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,

                >>::try_read(buf)?,
                include_externals: <bool as ::uniffi::Lift<
                    crate::UniFfiTag,
                >>::try_read(buf)?,
